    @pytest.mark.error
    def test_invalid_column_type_rejected(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path):
        """Error: Invalid column types should fail schema validation.
    
        The spec lists valid types as: INTEGER|TEXT|REAL|BLOB|TIMESTAMP
        Types like VARCHAR, BOOLEAN, INT should be rejected.
        """
        migration_file = tmp_path / "migration.json"
        db_file = tmp_path / "app.db"
    
        migration = {
            "version": 1,
            "description": "Table with invalid type",
            "operations": [
                {
                    "type": "create_table",
                    "table": "users",
                    "columns": [
                        {"name": "id", "type": "INTEGER", "primary_key": True},
                        {"name": "name", "type": "VARCHAR"},  # Invalid type!
                    ],
                }
            ],
        }
    
        migration_file.write_text(json.dumps(migration))
        result = run_command(
            entrypoint_argv, "migrate", str(migration_file), str(db_file)
        )

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_migrate_column_data_default_value_for_null(
        entrypoint_argv, seeds_dir, tmp_path
    ):
        """Functionality: Default value is used when from_column is NULL."""
        migration_file = tmp_path / "migration.json"
        db_file = tmp_path / "app.db"
        shutil.copy(seeds_dir / "checkpoint_2_users.db", db_file)
    
        with sqlite3.connect(db_file) as conn:
            conn.execute("UPDATE users SET old_email = NULL WHERE id = 1")
            conn.commit()
    
        migration = {
            "version": 2,
            "description": "Migrate emails with default for NULL",
            "operations": [
                {
                    "type": "add_column",
                    "table": "users",
                    "column": {"name": "new_email", "type": "TEXT", "not_null": False},
                },
                {
                    "type": "migrate_column_data",
                    "table": "users",
                    "from_column": "old_email",
                    "to_column": "new_email",
                    "default_value": "'unknown'",
                },
            ],
        }
    
        migration_file.write_text(json.dumps(migration))
    
        result = run_command(
            entrypoint_argv, "migrate", str(migration_file), str(db_file)
        )
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, f"Command failed: {result.stderr}"
        events = parse_jsonl(result.stdout)
        assert events == [
            {"event": "operation_applied", "type": "add_column", "table": "users", "column": "new_email", "version": 2},
            {"event": "operation_applied", "type": "migrate_column_data", "table": "users", "from_column": "old_email", "to_column": "new_email", "version": 2},
            {"event": "migration_complete", "version": 2, "operations_count": 2},
        ]
    
        with sqlite3.connect(db_file) as conn:
            rows = conn.execute(
                "SELECT id, old_email, new_email FROM users ORDER BY id"
            ).fetchall()

    def test_transform_target_column_not_exists(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path):

    @pytest.mark.error
    def test_transactional_rollback_on_failure(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path):
        """Error: Failed op should roll back prior operations."""
        migration_file = tmp_path / "migration.json"
        db_file = tmp_path / "app.db"
    
        with sqlite3.connect(db_file) as conn:
            conn.execute(
                "CREATE TABLE posts (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER)"
            )
            conn.commit()
    
        migration = {
            "version": 1,
            "description": "Create index then fail",
            "operations": [
                {
                    "type": "create_index",
                    "name": "idx_posts_user_id",
                    "table": "posts",
                    "columns": ["user_id"],
                    "unique": False,
                },
                {
                    "type": "add_foreign_key",
                    "table": "posts",
                    "name": "fk_posts_user_id",
                    "columns": ["user_id"],
                    "references": {"table": "users", "columns": ["id"]},
                },
            ],
        }
    
        migration_file.write_text(json.dumps(migration))
    
        result = run_command(
            entrypoint_argv, "migrate", str(migration_file), str(db_file)
        )
    
        assert result.returncode == 1, "Expected command to fail with exit code 1"
    
        with sqlite3.connect(db_file) as conn:
            indexes = conn.execute("PRAGMA index_list(posts)").fetchall()
            try:
                applied = conn.execute(
                    "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM _migrations WHERE version = 1"
                ).fetchone()[0]
            except sqlite3.OperationalError:
                applied = 0
>       assert all(idx[1] != "idx_posts_user_id" for idx in indexes)
